title: Hieracium siljense
taxonomy: Hieracium siljense is a species of plant in the dandelion genus Hieracium. It belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Hieracium siljense is further classified as a flowering plant within the subclass Rosidae. Within the family Asteraceae, Hieracium siljense is classified as a member of the subfamily Cichorioideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Hieracium siljense, also known as Siljan hawkweed, is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium siljense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium siljense is often prepared as a tincture or decoction, and is used to treat various ailments, including arthritis, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
